course.name: Acute Care Across the Lifespan for the Family Nurse Practitioner

children.text: Acute Care Across the Lifespan for the Family Nurse Practitioner

prepares family nurse practitioner students to develop comprehensive, evidence-based, patient/client-centered care plans to manage acute health conditions across the lifespan. The course emphasizes critical thinking, diagnostic reasoning, appropriate diagnostic testing, and prescribing both pharmacologic and non-pharmacologic therapies including health promotion, education, diagnosis, and consultation and referral processes. Completion of all MSN Core courses, along with Advanced Pathophysiology, Advanced Pharmacology, Advanced Health Assessment, and Health Promotion Across the Lifespan, is required before enrolling in this course.
The learning experience should include the following:
aligns with the Crisis Prevention Intervention (CPI) Certificate competencies.
aligns with the American Heart Association (AHA) TelePrimary Care certificate competencies.
of individuals with acute conditions across the lifespan.
Handling medical emergencies (beyond stabilization) in the primary care setting.

course.assessmentModalityRationale:
OA: We need to measure learners' understanding of established points of declarative knowledge and prepare for the licensure exam.    
PA: This will provide an authentic and comprehensive measure of competency. Students will demonstrate their ability to synthesize theory with practice, evaluate complex scenarios, and communicate solutions effectively.

children.text: PA with two tasks: 1) CPE and 2) summative:
Diagnostic gold standard and evidence-based treatment of the 20 most common acute care conditions.

The MS in Nursing, Family Nurse Practitioner involves the implementation of new technologies as well as use of existing tools. For an overview of the tools and technologies, refer to Technology Ecosystem table in the Program Vision and Strategy document.
should be aligned in this course. Certificates

value.name: Practice guidelines for family nurse practitioners

comps.titleAndStatement: Conduct Health Assessments to Identify Acute Health Conditions .  The learner conducts health assessments to identify acute health conditions for individuals across the lifespan.
comps.modalityRationale: This will provide an authentic and comprehensive measure of competency. Learners will demonstrate their ability to synthesize theory with practice, evaluate complex scenarios, and communicate solutions effectively.

children.text: AACN Essentials Concepts for Nursing Practice:

skills.title: Acute Illness Concepts
skills.statement: Recognize the basic concepts of acute illness.

comps.titleAndStatement: Applies Diagnostic Reasoning to Establish a Diagnosis for Acute Conditions .  The learner applies diagnostic reasoning to establish an evidence-based diagnosis for individuals with acute conditions across the lifespan.
comps.modalityRationale: We need to measure learners’ understanding of established points of declarative knowledge and prepare for the licensure exam.
comps.evidence: Scenario-based items that require learners to select the appropriate acute condition diagnosis based on diagnostic reasoning and evidence-based practice.

children.text: AACN Essentials Concepts for Nursing Practice:

skills.title: Care Plan Development
skills.statement: Develop a care plan for each patient.

skills.title: Medical Condition Diagnosis
skills.statement: Diagnose a patient’s medical condition based on a clinical evaluation.

comps.titleAndStatement: Creates Health Plans to Treat Acute Conditions .  The learner creates evidence-based health plans for treatment of acute conditions for individuals across the lifespan.
comps.modalityRationale: We need to measure learners’ understanding of established points of declarative knowledge and prepare for the licensure exam.
comps.evidence: Scenario-based items that require learners to select the appropriate evidence-based treatment plan for an acute condition.

children.text: AACN Essentials Concepts for Nursing Practice:

skills.title: Care Plan Evolution Prediction
skills.statement: Predict how a complex patient care plan may evolve as the patient moves through different stages of the developmental lifespan.

skills.title: Patient-Centered Care Plan Creation
skills.statement: Create a patient-centered care plan using information from the interdisciplinary team.

skills.title: Record Patient Data
skills.statement: Write patient assessments, treatment plans, and progress notes.

skills.title: Patient Care Documentation
skills.statement: Document patient care in an electronic medical record (EMR).

skills.title: Accurate Patient Information Maintenance
skills.statement: Maintain accurate patient demographic, health, diagnosis, and treatment plan information.

comps.titleAndStatement: Evaluates Acute Conditions Health Plans   .  The learner evaluates patient/client health plans for individuals across the lifespan with acute conditions.
comps.modalityRationale: We need to measure learners’ understanding of established points of declarative knowledge and prepare for the licensure exam.
comps.evidence: Scenario-based items that require learners to select the appropriate health plan for an acute condition.

children.text: AACN Essentials Concepts for Nursing Practice:

skills.title: Patient Complication Monitoring
skills.statement: Monitor patients for complications arising from treatments, diagnostics, procedures, and implementation of interventions to prevent negative outcomes.

comps.titleAndStatement: Communicates with Stakeholders to Ensure Coordinated Care for Acute Conditions .  The learner communicates with patients/clients, families, and healthcare teams to ensure coordinated and patient-centered care for acute conditions.
comps.modalityRationale: We need to measure learners’ understanding of established points of declarative knowledge and prepare for the licensure exam.
comps.evidence: Scenario-based items that require learners to select the appropriate communication to ensure coordinated and patient-centered care for an acute condition.

children.text: AACN Essentials Concepts for Nursing Practice:

skills.title: Patient Rights Education
skills.statement: Educate patients regarding patient rights, medications, treatments, testing, and treatment plans.

skills.title: Medical Diagnosis Communication
skills.statement: Communicate medical diagnoses and the treatments clearly and with compassion.

skills.title: Multidisciplinary Team Treatment Plan Discussion
skills.statement: Discuss a treatment plan with healthcare professionals in multiple disciplines before discussing a treatment plan with a patient.
